fault_hypothesis: Line 67 uses strict greater-than (`>`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`>=`), so a batch whose size exactly equals max_jobs is incorrectly admitted instead of rejected.

constraints.intent: When batch_size equals max_jobs the condition must be True (reject), but the strict > makes it False, so the return value will be wrong.
When batch_size equals max_jobs it should satisfy the >= threshold used for rejection.
Immediately before the capacity check, a batch of exactly max_jobs should be treated as at-or-over capacity.
In the else branch (batch accepted), the batch size must be strictly less than max_jobs; equality should have triggered rejection.
